items.name: fucking-awesome-machine-learning
items.description: A curated list of awesome Machine Learning frameworks, libraries and software. With repository stars⭐ and forks🍴

items.name: used-car-price-prediction
items.description: Machine Learning project using Linear Regression to predict used car prices following the CRISP-DM framework.

items.name: quantum
items.description: An open-source Python framework for hybrid quantum-classical machine learning.

items.name: aagjuuk-core
items.description: Scientific Machine Learning (SciML) framework utilizing Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs) to solve transient non-linear partial differential equations for real-time industrial simulation.

items.name: ml-nuclear-materials
items.description: Machine learning frameworks and data pipelines for modeling microstructural evolution, radiation damage, and mechanical degradation in nuclear materials.

items.name: NEGI-Framework
items.description: A remote sensing, machine learning, and energy assessment framework for evaluating urban greening energy trade-offs in desalination-dependent cities.

items.name: mlgovernance
items.description: Projeto para experimentar padrões e frameworks de governança de machine learning

items.name: IntegratedPipeline-Specialized-Non-LLM-AI-Agent-Framework
items.description: [~]  IntegratedPipeline is a standalone AI Agent Library for Non-LLM memory Augmented Agentic Framework, Specifically designed to provide Agentic capability for any Autonomous Agentic Framework locally and Coordinatively that runs efficiently on Consumer based machine to High end embedded systems, While continuously learning and runs efficiently.

items.name: quantitative-option-research-engine
items.description: Developing a modular quantitative research engine in R that evaluates option strategies using Monte Carlo simulation, real option chain data, and statistical modeling. The framework generates, evaluates, and ranks strategies based on expected return and risk, with future plans for backtesting and machine learning.

items.name: DriveValue-AI
items.description: An Explainable Machine Learning Framework for Predicting Used Car Prices using Python and Scikit-Learn.

items.name: FEDOT

items.name: DeepGNN
items.description: DeepGNN is a framework for training machine learning models on large scale graph data.

items.name: Blockchain-XAI-Diabetes-Prediction
items.description: Blockchain-enabled explainable machine learning framework for diabetes risk prediction using healthcare data. Evaluates 7 ML models (LR, k-NN, SVM, DT, RF, GBM, NB) with SHAP/LIME explanations and Solidity smart contract logging on Ganache blockchain.

items.name: ALS-Speech-Tremor-Detection
items.description: Machine learning framework for early diagnosis of Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis (ALS) using speech tremor analysis from sustained phonation recordings. Published in Engineering Research Express (IOP Publishing).

items.name: scalable-inference-serving
items.description: A collection of APIs, frameworks, and platforms for scalable machine learning model inference serving, deployment, and management.

items.name: pytorch
items.description: APIs and resources for PyTorch, an open source machine learning framework for tensor computation and deep learning developed by Meta.

items.name: amazon-emr
items.description: Amazon EMR is a cloud big data platform for running large-scale distributed data processing jobs, interactive SQL queries, and machine learning applications using open-source analytics frameworks such as Apache Spark, Apache Hive, Apache HBase, Apache Flink, Apache Hudi, and Presto.

items.name: google-tensorflow
items.description: Google TensorFlow is an open-source machine learning framework providing APIs and tools for building, training, and deploying ML models, including TensorFlow Serving for model inference and TensorFlow Hub for reusable model components.

items.name: apache-mahout
items.description: Apache Mahout is an open-source framework for building scalable machine learning applications.

items.name: streamlit
items.description: Streamlit is an open-source Python framework that makes it easy to build and share beautiful, custom web apps for machine learning and data science. With Streamlit, you can turn data scripts into shareable web applications in minutes without needing front-end experience.

items.name: zenml
items.description: ZenML is an open-source MLOps and LLMOps framework that unifies machine learning and generative AI workflows through a single orchestration, versioning, and governance layer.

items.name: ML-Glacier-SMB-SEB
items.description: Machine learning framework for modeling glacier surface mass balance (SMB) and surface energy balance (SEB) using COSIPY output and ERA5-Land..

items.name: Apache-Spark-Installation-on-Windows
items.description: Apache Spark is a fast, open-source distributed computing framework designed for large-scale data processing and analytics. It provides high-level APIs for Python, Java, Scala, and R, enabling efficient batch processing, streaming, machine learning, and graph analytics.

items.name: refiner
items.description: Refiner by Macrodata Labs, a data processing framework for Machine Learning large scale datasets

items.name: Hybrid-Quantum-Classical-Supercomputing-Framework
items.description: A hybrid quantum-classical framework for energy load forecasting that combines Linear Regression with the Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm (QAOA) using Qiskit, demonstrating the integration of quantum optimization into machine learning workflows.

items.name: Credit-Worthiness-ML-Classifier
items.description: Machine learning framework for bank credit worthiness prediction using supervised classification algorithms and class imbalance handling techniques.

items.name: Apache-Spark-Installation-on-Windows

items.description: An AI-driven, data-centric framework for dynamic customer segmentation. This research synthesizes 24 million credit card transactions into 12 actionable lifestyle personas using K-Means clustering, featuring t-SNE validation and Explainable AI (XAI) methodology for transparent product strategy.

The project features automated preprocessing, model evaluation, visualization, and performance comparison on a large-scale clinical dataset (200K

items.name: Linear-Regression

items.name: Timeseries-Forecasting-Sales-data
items.description: This repository delivers a robust framework for time series forecasting, comparing traditional statistical methods against modern machine learning algorithms. By benchmarking SARIMA, SARIMAX, Random Forest, and XGBoost, this project highlights the trade-offs between linear statistical rigor and non-linear, feature-driven machine learning models.

items.name: scikit-learn_bench
items.description: scikit-learn_bench benchmarks various implementations of machine learning algorithms across data analytics frameworks. It currently support the scikit-learn, DAAL4PY, cuML, and XGBoost frameworks for commonly used machine learning algorithms.

items.name: Olist-cltv-prediction
items.description: A 90-day Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) forecasting framework comparing BTYD probabilistic models (BG/NBD, Gamma-Gamma) with Machine Learning (XGBoost) on the Olist dataset..

Includes risk-tiered hypothesis testing to optimize lending decisions.

The project utilizes XGBoost optimized with Optuna and provides interpretability via SHAP values to analyze feature importance compared to existing benchmarks.
